skill.name: opentui
skill.description: Guides agents through building OpenTUI terminal user interfaces with the core API, React reconciler, Solid reconciler, layout, input, animation, and testing references.
skill.summary: Build OpenTUI terminal interfaces with framework-specific references and implementation guidance.
skill.author: msmps

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many command, filesystem, network, and credential-pattern hits, but review found they are Markdown documentation examples, not executable skill code. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The skill is publishable with a medium warning because it guides agents to run Bun package commands and create local files during legitimate OpenTUI development.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Guided Package Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends Bun and create-tui commands for project setup. This is legitimate for OpenTUI development, but agents may execute network-backed package installation commands in the user workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real development commands in Markdown, not hidden execution. The risk is contextual because an agent using the skill may run them during setup.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples
medium_findings.description: Several references show reading API_URL, API_KEY, DEV, and OpenTUI debug variables. The examples are normal configuration guidance, but users should avoid exposing secrets in generated code or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access is shown as documentation, and no exfiltration path was found. It still involves potentially sensitive variables in examples, so publication should carry a warning.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most Ruby backtick and external command detections are caused by inline code spans, code fences, and shell examples in documentation. No executable Ruby, JavaScript, or shell wrapper was found in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown reference material, and the patterns are formatting around commands and API names. The skill contains no script file that automatically executes these snippets.

low_findings.title: Filesystem and Path Traversal Detections Are Documentation Paths
low_findings.description: Path traversal and file operation detections are examples of relative documentation links, test paths, and debug logging snippets. They do not implement file traversal logic in the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are Markdown cross-references or illustrative application snippets. No skill code accepts user-controlled paths or reads arbitrary files.

low_findings.title: Network Detections Are Example URLs
low_findings.description: Network findings point to documentation links and example fetch calls using api.example.com. No evidence found that the skill sends data to external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public documentation resources or placeholder API examples. There is no surrounding code that collects secrets or transmits workspace data.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Malicious Instructions
low_findings.description: No prompt-injection language was found. The only override-related hit was the documented OpenTUI variable OTUI_OVERRIDE_STDOUT, which is not an instruction to alter audit behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found no phrases asking the evaluator to ignore instructions, skip analysis, or change risk levels. The remaining hit is a product configuration variable name.

content.value_statement: OpenTUI projects require framework-specific choices, terminal layout rules, and runtime details. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured reference for building reliable terminal interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Selects the right OpenTUI framework path: core, React, or Solid.
Provides quick-start commands for create-tui and manual Bun setup.
Explains layout, keyboard handling, input components, text display, and animation references.
Guides testing with OpenTUI test renderer concepts and snapshot workflows.
Highlights runtime gotchas such as terminal cleanup, focus handling, and text styling rules.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not include executable tooling.
Examples assume Bun and OpenTUI project conventions.
It does not replace upstream OpenTUI API documentation for version-specific changes.
Generated projects may still need manual testing in a real terminal.

use_cases.title: Start a New TUI App
use_cases.description: Choose core, React, or Solid and scaffold an OpenTUI project with the correct create-tui options.

use_cases.title: Implement Terminal Components
use_cases.description: Build screens with text, boxes, inputs, tables, code views, diffs, and markdown rendering.

use_cases.title: Debug and Test TUI Behavior
use_cases.description: Investigate focus, layout, cleanup, console output, and snapshot testing issues in OpenTUI apps.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenTUI skill to choose between core, React, and Solid for a terminal app that needs [requirements]. Explain the tradeoffs and next files to read.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Screen Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenTUI references to design a terminal screen with [sections]. Include layout approach, component choices, and key runtime notes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Interactive Input
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the OpenTUI skill to add [input behavior] to an existing [core, React, or Solid] app. Include focus handling and keyboard shortcuts.

prompt_templates.title: Review a TUI Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this OpenTUI implementation for layout, terminal cleanup, input handling, testing gaps, and framework-specific gotchas: [paste summary or files].

output_examples.input: Plan a React OpenTUI dashboard with a list, detail pane, and command input.
output: Recommended framework: React reconciler for familiar component state and declarative layout.
Read order: React overview, React API, components for containers and inputs, then layout patterns.
Implementation outline: root vertical container, split content row, focused command input, and cleanup through renderer destroy.

output_examples.input: Troubleshoot why a text input does not receive keystrokes.
output: Likely issue: the input component is rendered but not focused.
Check the keyboard reference and input component docs for focus management.
Add a focused state path and verify behavior with an interaction test.

output_examples.input: Add tests for a terminal component with changing output.
output: Use the testing reference for renderer setup and snapshot expectations.
Stabilize dynamic values before asserting output.
Add one interaction test for keyboard behavior and one snapshot for final render state.

best_practices: Start with the framework REFERENCE.md file before reading deeper API pages.
Use create-tui with the template flag before the project name for new projects.
Test cleanup, focus behavior, and terminal rendering before shipping a TUI.
anti_patterns: Do not call process.exit before the renderer has cleaned up the terminal.
Do not use interactive create-tui prompts when an agent must run the command autonomously.
Do not assume React and Solid component names are identical in every case.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps agents build OpenTUI terminal applications using core, React, or Solid references.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete application?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but it is a reference skill and not a project generator by itself.

faq.question: Which runtime does it assume?
faq.answer: The references assume Bun because OpenTUI is built around Bun workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with keyboard input?
faq.answer: Yes. It points to keyboard events, focus handling, paste handling, and shortcut guidance.

faq.question: Can it help test terminal UIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes references for test renderer setup, snapshots, and interaction testing.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The documentation includes package commands, environment variable examples, network examples, and file logging snippets.
